title: iopadmap is confused by loops
body:
Here's a design I have, which, albeit unusual, seems entirely legal to me:
```verilog
module top(
    inout  a,
    inout  b,
    input  dir,
);

    wire aout, bout;
    GP_IOBUF iobufa(
        .IO(a),
        .OUT(aout),
        .IN(bout),
        .OE(dir)
    );
    GP_IOBUF iobufb(
        .IO(b),
        .OUT(bout),
        .IN(aout),
        .OE(!dir)
    );

endmodule
```

When I run:
```
yosys top.v -p "synth_greenpak4 -part SLG46621V; show"
```
I get:
![screenshot_20180404_114903](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54771/38306526-607ae2ca-3800-11e8-8d36-ad8e2e600c26.png)
which is obviously wrong IMO.

If, instead, I alter the pipeline to only apply the iopadmap pass to `dir`, then I get:
![screenshot_20180404_121023](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54771/38306869-6a160dd6-3801-11e8-9ea5-85fc291196cc.png)
which produces the correct post-PAR netlist.
